title: uxlistbox
text: uxlistbox The uxlistbox widget is a control element that displays a list of items and allows the user to select a single option from a short list. You can use uxlistbox in data-entry constructions next to a radiogroup and/or a textfield. Note: As there are limitations in the current version of the fluent-listbox , you can select only single item at a time in the uxlistbox widget. If a user wants to create a scenario where they need to allow selection of multiple options, they can use a construction based on uxcheckbox . For more details, see uxcheckbox . As uxlistbox widget is always vertically oriented, you must use it in the layouts that offer vertical scrolling space. Default Logical Widgets The default logical widget is ux-ListBox . Triggers (web)trigger onchange : This trigger is fired when the fluent-option of the fluent-listbox is pressed. Extends fluent-listbox Web Component This widget is implemented using the Fluent web component fluent-listbox . Most of its features are also available in this widget. Most of its events are mapped to triggers. For more information, see the Fluent documentation on the fluent-listbox web component. Keyboard support Pressing the Tab key enters the listbox Vertical scrolling keys navigate and select an item from the listbox Pressing the Tab key again exits the listbox Layout processing Widget class method processLayout() constructs the template HTML for this widget. It generates nodes for the control itself as well as slots for label and error icon, and returns it to Uniface. Use this function to enhance the widget with more static features. Widget properties The uxlistbox widget supports the following properties: Property Id Description html:title Defines a hover over text or tooltip, currently used as helper text. html:hidden Defines a boolean value that hides or shows a listbox. html:disabled Defines the widget as being disabled. html:readonly Defines the widget as being read-only. label-text Defines the label text. Note: If label text is defined, the label appears by default above the widget. Clicking the label sets focus to the fluent-listbox after which arrow keys can be used to navigate the fluent-options. If not defined, no label is shown. class:classname Boolean value that adds or removes a specified class. html:tabindex Allows or prevents a field from getting sequential focus usually with the Tab key. size Defines the maximum height of the listbox, allowing the scrollbar to appear when exceeded. display-format Defines how values are displayed by the widget. The available options are: rep : Displays the representation only. This is a default value. val : Displays the value only. valrep : Displays both value and representation. exclude Defines that there will be no column for the field in the uxdatagrid . Note: This is actually not a widget property. It is a field property, used by the uxdatagrid widget if that is the containing entity's widget. Helper and validation error visualization A uxlistbox widget deals with the following error visualizations: Alert icon Tool-tip when hovering the "Alert icon" showing the error message When the uxlistbox has focus, no red border is shown, only error icon is shown A no-representation-error error is shown if the field value does not have a corresponding representation. This includes a null value if the property is not set. UI blocking When the field is in transaction, the uxlistbox widget blocks user input by becoming read-only. Widget styling CSS Selector Selected node(s) .u-listbox Widget node .u-listbox .u-option Valrep option node .u-listbox.disabled or .u-listbox[disabled] Widget node when it is set to disabled .u-listbox.readonly or .u-listbox[readonly] Widget node when it is set to read-only .u-listbox:focus Widget node when it has focus Note: This applies to tabular-focus on keyboard navigation. .u-listbox .u-label-text Label text node slot:not([name]) Fluent option slot node u-listbox .u-error-icon Error icon node Widget sources The widget source files, such as JavaScript and CSS files, can be downloaded from the following GitHub repository: https://github.com/RocketSoftware/uniface-ux/tree/main/src/ux/listbox . Related Links ux-ListBox This section provides description for the logical UX widget ux-ListBox properties. UX Widgets Uniface UX provides a set of widgets that are implemented using the UX Widget Interface. This set is referred as UX Widgets. The sources of these widgets are available and allow changes and/or extensions to the widgets. In a default Uniface installation, you can find the sources in the <install>\uniface\webapps\uniface\common\ux folder. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
